title: Bias Atlas — cognitive-bias catalogue grounded in Heuer and Kahneman

fields.vision: A catalogued atlas of cognitive biases that affect analytical work.
fields.mechanism: Four coordinated lenses on the bias canon — biases catalogued by analytical lane, the scholarly foundation each bias is drawn from, and the mitigation grammar that maps bias to mitigating analytical move. Reading the atlas is a sequence: identify the bias, read its foundation, follow the mitigation map.
fields.built_components: The atlas is structurally complete on the public site. Biases are catalogued by analytical lane, the Heuer and Kahneman foundations are present, and the bias-to-mitigating-SAT mapping is in place. Open work: the binding from each catalogued bias to its specific admitted Heuer or Kahneman passage has not yet been wired through the source-register pipeline. The bias names and the mitigation suggestions are accurate; the citation chain to the underlying source passages is the next phase.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Graduate the atlas from a structurally-complete reference into a fully source-bound reference — every bias citation traceable to a specific Heuer or Kahneman passage, every mitigation traceable to a specific SAT in the canonical primer. Step 01 Bias-citation binding Every bias entry bound to its Heuer or Kahneman passage through the source-register pipeline. Step 02 Mitigation-citation binding Every mitigation traceable to a specific SAT in the canonical primer. Step 03 Consult-before-claiming page Atlas becomes the consult-before-claiming page for any analytical work elsewhere in the portfolio.
fields.status: Status: The atlas is structurally complete on the public site. Biases are catalogued by analytical lane, the Heuer and Kahneman foundations are present, and the bias-to-mitigating-SAT mapping is in place. Open work: the binding from each catalogued bias to its specific admitted Heuer or Kahneman passage has not yet been wired through the source-register pipeline. The bias names and the mitigation suggestions are accurate; the citation chain to the underlying source passages is the next phase.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: graduate the atlas from a structurally-complete reference into a fully source-bound reference — every bias citation traceable to a specific Heuer or Kahneman passage, every mitigation traceable to a specific SAT in the canonical primer. Next: Bias-citation binding Every bias entry bound to its Heuer or Kahneman passage through the source-register pipeline.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A catalogued atlas of cognitive biases that affect analytical work. Pairs each bias with its scholarly foundation (the Heuer, Kahneman, or SAT-canon source it derives from) and with the mitigating analytical move that the literature recommends for it. The atlas is structurally a reference shelf — distinct from the interactive bias-detection page in the Analyst Workstation suite, which exercises the same canon as a working surface. The atlas is what the analyst consults; the challenge page is what the analyst trains against.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Four coordinated lenses on the bias canon — biases catalogued by analytical lane, the scholarly foundation each bias is drawn from, and the mitigation grammar that maps bias to mitigating analytical move. Reading the atlas is a sequence: identify the bias, read its foundation, follow the mitigation map.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.

public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.
